articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

images.name: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
images.caption: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

articles.headline: Messi: It's a joy to continue at Inter Miami

images.caption: Lionel Messi says he's "very happy" to continue at Inter Miami after signing a new deal with the club.

articles.headline: Carlo Ancelotti speaks about life as Brazil's head coach

article.headline: Luis Díaz scores two in Colombia's comeback win over Brazil
article.description: Brazil lost their second consecutive World Cup qualifier match as Colombia fought back to win 2-1 against the five-times champions, who are winless in the last three games and dropped to fifth place in the South American standings on Thursday.
article.linkText: Colombia beats Brazil behind Luis Diaz's brace

images.name: Colombia boss praises 'hero' goal scorer Luis Diaz
images.caption: Nestor Lorenzo lauds Luis Diaz after his goals in front of his watching father earned Colombia a 2-1 win vs. Brazil.

video.headline: Colombia boss praises 'hero' goal scorer Luis Diaz
video.caption: Nestor Lorenzo lauds Luis Diaz after his goals in front of his watching father earned Colombia a 2-1 win vs. Brazil.
video.title: Colombia boss praises 'hero' goal scorer Luis Diaz
video.description: Nestor Lorenzo lauds Luis Diaz after his goals in front of his watching father earned Colombia a 2-1 win vs. Brazil.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (COL) (Colombia boss praises hero goal scorer Luis Diaz) 20231117 GLOBAL

article.story:
Brazil lost their second consecutive World Cup qualifier match as Colombia fought back to win 2-1 against the five-times champions, who are winless in the last three games and dropped to fifth place in the South American standings on Thursday.

It was a cathartic evening for Liverpool forward Luis Diaz who, just a few days after Colombia guerrillas freed his kidnapped father, scored twice within four minutes late in the second-half to give his country the shock win, after Brazil got an early lead with a Gabriel Martinelli strike.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Brazil started well and dominated early proceedings, taking the lead in the fourth minute through a strike by Gabriel Martinelli, assisted by Vinicius Junior's fine first touch pass.

The jet-heeled Real Madrid winger was forced out due to a thigh injury halfway through the first-half after which the home side slowly took control, roared on by the crowd at the sold-out Metropolitano stadium in Barranquilla.

Luis Díaz alone created 10 attempts on target for Colombia, but was denied the equaliser by a string of fine saves from his Liverpool team mate Alisson, before defensive mistakes helped him net two headers in the 75th and 79th minutes.

Diaz burst into tears as he dedicated the win to his father who was celebrating emotionally in the stands.

Winless in the last three games, Brazil will be under heavy pressure as they are set to host long-time rivals Argentina at the iconic Maracana in Rio de Janeiro on Tuesday.

Colombia is third on nine points, one ahead Venezuela who was held to a goal less draw at home by sixth-placed Ecuador, who is one point behind Brazil that is fifth on seven points.

videos.headline: Colombia boss praises 'hero' goal scorer Luis Diaz
videos.description: Nestor Lorenzo lauds Luis Diaz after his goals in front of his watching father earned Colombia a 2-1 win vs. Brazil.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (COL) (Colombia boss praises hero goal scorer Luis Diaz) 20231117 GLOBAL
